sources.notes: Official tutorial derives the answer by classifying row/column contributions and applying a closed-form count.

annotation.summary: Count valid grid configurations satisfying the local/global constraints from the statement.
annotation.constraints: The grid dimensions can be large, so the solution needs a formula rather than iterating cells.
annotation.core_idea: Break the grid into independent row/column contribution types. After classifying the few boundary/symmetry cases, the number of valid grids is a product/sum of simple combinatorial terms. Implementation mainly requires handling degenerate dimensions separately.

solution_variants.name: closed-form-grid-count
solution_variants.summary: Classify grid cases and evaluate the corresponding formula.

tags.evidence: The accepted solution is a direct combinatorial counting formula over grid cases.
Most risk is in handling small/degenerate grid dimensions and modular formula details.
